# Design doc outline

> Use this as a menu, not a checklist. Include only the sections this change
> actually needs — a small or well-understood change may need just a few. Add a
> section for security, API or interface contracts, performance, reliability,
> compliance, or specialized environments only when that concern carries real
> weight here; otherwise leave it out. Keep it lightweight and the tone plain.

## Overview

_A short summary of what this design is about._

## Problem statement

_The problem being solved and for whom._

## Goals

_What this design intends to achieve._

## Non-goals

_What is explicitly out of scope._

## Proposed solution

_The chosen approach._

## Alternatives considered

_Other options and why they were not chosen._

## Trade-offs

_What is gained and what is given up._

## Risks and mitigations

_What could go wrong and how it is addressed._

## User-facing implications

_How the design affects users, operators, and documentation._

## Testing and validation

_How correctness and quality will be confirmed._

## Rollout or migration considerations

_How the change reaches users and how existing users adopt it._

## Open questions

_Unresolved points and decisions still needing an owner._

## Appendix

_Supporting detail, references, and background._
